direct_hit_doors: ahacker-1/cre-agent-skills: CRE Due Diligence plugin pack

top_candidates.why_it_matters: Closest direct match for public records, ownership, liens, litigation, zoning, code violations, permits, certificates of occupancy, sources, data quality notes, and uncertainty flags.
top_candidates.what_to_copy: Public-record gates for code violations, permits, occupancy certificates, zoning, litigation, source coverage, and uncertainty flags.
top_candidates.what_to_ignore: US-specific title insurance and county/state terminology unless localized.

top_candidates.why_it_matters: Directly names construction permits, building code, inspections, occupancy, environmental compliance, document/submittal workflows, jurisdiction caveats, and evidence-referenced findings.
top_candidates.what_to_copy: Permit type taxonomy, certificate/inspection coverage, document evidence categories, and jurisdiction-specific caveat language.
top_candidates.what_to_ignore: Software-platform audit framing; adapt to public-record source evidence.

top_candidates.why_it_matters: Best pattern for evidence packet intake: classify documents, extract fields, detect missing documents, surface conflicts, score confidence, and require confirmation for ambiguity.
top_candidates.what_to_copy: Intake inventory, classification confidence, completeness scoring, conflict detection, missing-document list, and user-confirmation boundary.
top_candidates.what_to_ignore: CRE-specific financial document categories unless needed as optional evidence.

top_candidates.why_it_matters: Strong source-taxonomy and hazard-context pattern: database search matrix, missing-data handling, flood/hazard checks, source list, and uncertainty flags.
top_candidates.what_to_copy: Source taxonomy, conservative missing-data protocol, public database matrix, hazard context, source list, and validation gates.
top_candidates.what_to_ignore: US-specific environmental databases, cost assumptions, and acquisition underwriting language.

top_candidates.why_it_matters: Good building-system checklist and missing-evidence model, with explicit boundary that structural, seismic, fire/life-safety, and condition findings require qualified review.
top_candidates.what_to_copy: Building systems checklist, permit/photo/review evidence gathering, conservative assumptions, issue flags, and uncertainty handling.
top_candidates.what_to_ignore: Renovation ROI, CapEx underwriting, and investment framing.

top_candidates.why_it_matters: Useful for exception classification, cross-source validation, legal description matching, survey/title inconsistencies, risk flags, and public workaround sources.
top_candidates.what_to_copy: Exception classification, cross-reference validation, risk flags, uncertainty flags, and human-review boundary.
top_candidates.what_to_ignore: ALTA/title endorsement mechanics unless mapped to a local equivalent.

top_candidates.why_it_matters: Provides vocabulary tags for Search, Aggregation, Transformation, Compliance, Data, Storage, Geolocation, Government, and Real Estate.
top_candidates.what_to_copy: Router vocabulary and source taxonomy labels.
top_candidates.what_to_ignore: Any assumption that a vocabulary term proves a source exists.

top_candidates.why_it_matters: Useful for severity labels, risk bands, automatic escalation rules, and mitigation output, as long as it remains caveated and human-reviewed.
top_candidates.what_to_copy: Risk bands, automatic escalation triggers, mitigation fields, and materiality review.
top_candidates.what_to_ignore: CRE investment weights and dealbreaker framing.

pilot_takeaways: The router worked: it led agents to a small set of high-value doors instead of expanding the entire 171-door catalog.
The strongest direct source for the earthquake/building evidence workflow is ahacker-1/cre-agent-skills, especially due-diligence, legal, and document-ingestion.
tinh2/skills-hub-registry contributes a directly named permit-compliance door and useful compliance orchestration, but it needs adaptation away from software audit framing.
api-evangelist/use-cases is best used as vocabulary and taxonomy, not workflow implementation.
The large catalog repos are useful for inspiration, but they should not be primary architecture sources for this workflow.
recommended_next_step: Build a curated earthquake/public-evidence workflow pattern pack from the top copy/adapt candidates before scaling to other domains.
